summary: Cleaned up mnSoundTest and mnDataDel menu data by splitting large anonymous .data blobs into typed objects, arrays, vectors, text IDs, and string labels, then updating C/header code to use those explicit symbols. The PR had no body and no human review comments, so intent is inferred from the diff and decomp.dev output. Automated results reported +280 matched code bytes, +1808 matched data bytes, 3 new matches, and 100% .data for both main/melee/mn/mndatadel and main/melee/mn/mnsoundtest.

key_files.role: Split mnSoundTest and mnDataDel .data ranges into smaller typed symbols; adjusted local/global scope for several bss/sdata2 symbols.
Shrank MnDataDelData, added static initializers for AnimLoopSettings, u32/u16 data arrays, and Vec3 position data; converted user-data flag bytes into x3[6].
Updated code from struct-field and pointer-cast indexing to direct typed arrays/symbols such as mnDataDel_803EF8AC, mnDataDel_803EF8C8, mnDataDel_803EF888, mnDataDel_803EF894, and lbl_803EF8D4.
Added vec_8, changed data_4 to s32, corrected data_3/data_4 indexing, changed one input check to MenuInput_StartButton, and returned HSD_GObjProc* directly.
Changed mnSoundTest_8024BEE0 prototype from s32 to HSD_GObjProc*.

what_changed: In symbols.txt, mnSoundTest_803EF0A8 was replaced by vec_0 through vec_8, and mnSoundTest_803EF114 was split into text_ids, data_2, data_3, data_4, and several mnSoundTest_803EF4F4-style string symbols.
In symbols.txt, mnDataDel_803EF870 was reduced from a 0x30 object to smaller AnimLoopSettings-like float objects at mnDataDel_803EF870, mnDataDel_803EF888, mnDataDel_803EF894, and mnDataDel_803EF8A0.

The previous large mnDataDel_803EF8AC data blob was split into mnDataDel_803EF8AC as u32 data, mnDataDel_803EF8C8 as u16 data, lbl_803EF8D4 as Vec3-like float data, and multiple string labels from lbl_803EF8E0 through lbl_803EFA60.
MnDataDelGObjUserData changed from separate bytes x3 through x8 plus padding to a contiguous u8 x3[6] flag array, and mndatadel.c now uses x3[i] or x3[n] consistently.

mndatadel.c replaced casts such as ((s32*) &mnDataDel_803EF870.x3C)[i] and ((u16*) &mnDataDel_803EF870.x58)[idx] with direct typed array access through mnDataDel_803EF8AC[i] and mnDataDel_803EF8C8[idx].
mndatadel.c now references standalone animation settings mnDataDel_803EF888, mnDataDel_803EF894, and mnDataDel_803EF8A0 instead of fields x18, x24, and x30 in a larger data struct.
mnDataDel_8024EA6C was rewritten from pointer-walk/do-while style to a for loop over six entries, with lang typed as enum_t and the gmMainLib_8015F588 argument cast to u8.
mnDataDel_8024FE4C now zeroes the flag array with ARRAY_SIZE, iterates over ARRAY_SIZE(user_data->x10), adds child joints through mnDataDel_803EF8AC[i], and reloads user_data before updating text.
mnsoundtest.c added Vec3 vec_8 = { -1.7, 2, 0 } and changed two text-positioning sites to use vec_8 values.
mnsoundtest.c changed data_4 from u32[] to s32[], changed mnSoundTest_8024BCA0 to use data_3[user_data->unk3] and data_4[temp_r29_3], and simplified a boolean argument to temp_r29_2 == 0.
mnsoundtest.c changed one input branch from MenuInput_LTrigger to MenuInput_StartButton in fn_8024AED0.
mnSoundTest_8024BEE0 now returns HSD_GObjProc* in both the C file and header instead of casting the proc pointer to s32.
smart_moves: The main matching win was splitting heterogeneous data blobs into correctly typed pieces instead of forcing unrelated vectors, animation settings, text IDs, strings, and integer tables into one synthetic struct.
Turning x3 through x8 into x3[6] made the data-delete menu state match the repeated indexing pattern already present in the code and removed manual byte-offset writes.
Replacing pointer casts against struct subfields with real u32 and u16 arrays made the code clearer and gave the compiler more direct indexed accesses.
Keeping uncertain names generic, such as vec_0, vec_8, data_2, data_3, data_4, and address-based mnDataDel_803EFxxx labels, avoided inventing semantics while still improving symbol/data accuracy.
The return type correction for mnSoundTest_8024BEE0 removed a pointer-to-s32 cast and aligned the declaration with the value actually returned by HSD_GObj_SetupProc.
The symbols.txt changes captured object sizes, data kinds, alignment, and local scope; this was enough for decomp.dev to report both affected .data units at 100%.
decomp_lessons: For menu data, do not assume a contiguous .data span is one logical struct; inspect relocations and uses to split it into AnimLoopSettings, Vec3, text ID arrays, integer remap arrays, and strings.
If several adjacent byte fields are manipulated with indexes, model them as an array even if the current field names are still offset-style placeholders.
Typed static data in a .static.h can be a practical bridge: it preserves address-based names while allowing C code to stop using brittle casts into oversized extern blobs.
Correct C types can be small but important matching levers: this PR used enum_t, u8 casts, s32 arrays, HSD_GObjProc* returns, and explicit u16 text ID arrays.
ARRAY_SIZE loops are useful when the recovered structure actually has array shape; they reduced manual initialization in MnDataDelGObjUserData without losing matching.
Automated match reports are valuable evidence for cleanup PRs with little prose: here the bot tied data-symbol cleanup to concrete code/data byte improvements.
naming_conventions: Unknown but now-separated mnSoundTest data used generic names vec_0 through vec_8, text_ids, data_2, data_3, and data_4 rather than over-specific inferred names.
Unidentified data-delete globals kept address-based labels such as mnDataDel_803EF888, mnDataDel_803EF894, mnDataDel_803EF8AC, mnDataDel_803EF8C8, and lbl_803EF8D4.
Compiler/local labels in symbols.txt use forms such as @8, @156, @203, and ...bss.0 when the symbol should not be promoted to a named global.
Offset-style struct field names were preserved where semantics are still unknown, but repeated bytes were grouped as x3[6] instead of x3, x4, x5, x6, x7, x8.
Function prototypes should reflect actual returned object types; mnSoundTest_8024BEE0 is now declared as returning HSD_GObjProc*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Symbol-map segmentation changed mnSoundTest_803EF0A8 from one 0x6C float object into nine 0xC Vec3-like objects and split mnSoundTest_803EF114 into tables and strings.
Symbol-map segmentation changed mnDataDel_803EF8AC from one 0x1DC 4-byte blob into a 0x1C u32 table, a 0xC u16 table, a Vec3-like float object, and multiple string objects.
mndatadel.c local declarations and padding were adjusted in mnDataDel_8024EA6C, including u8 pad[0x8] and PAD_STACK(0x10), which appears to be part of preserving the target stack/register layout.
Direct references to mnDataDel_803EF888, mnDataDel_803EF894, and mnDataDel_803EF8A0 replaced accesses through removed struct fields, reducing artificial offset arithmetic.
Using mnDataDel_803EF8AC[i] for joint indices and mnDataDel_803EF8C8[idx] for SIS text IDs avoided casts such as ((s32*) &data->x3C) and ((u16*) &data->x58).
mnsoundtest matching improved through small expression/type changes: data_4 became s32[], mnSoundTest_8024BCA0 uses data_3 then data_4, and a negated integer idiom became temp_r29_2 == 0.

decomp.dev reported new matches for mndatadel .data, mnsoundtest .data, and mnSoundTest_8024A958, plus improvements in mnDataDel_80250170, fn_8024F840, mnDataDel_8024FE4C, mnSoundTest_8024BCA0, mnDataDel_8024EA6C, fn_8024FD40, fn_8024B2B0, fn_8024ECCC, and fn_8024AED0.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
The PR body was empty, so rationale beyond the title is inferred from code changes and the automated match report.
decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code at 66.56% (+0.01%, +280 bytes) and matched data at 39.00% (+0.15%, +1808 bytes).
decomp-dev[bot] reported 3 new matches: main/melee/mn/mndatadel .data to 100.00%, main/melee/mn/mnsoundtest .data to 100.00%, and mnSoundTest_8024A958 to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Search nearby mn menu files for large address-based .data blobs that combine AnimLoopSettings, Vec3, text IDs, and strings and may benefit from this same split-symbol tactic.
Look for remaining casts like ((s32*) &data->field)[i] or ((u16*) &data->field)[i] in menu code; they may indicate missing typed arrays.
If more semantics are recovered, rename vec_0..vec_8, data_2, data_3, data_4, mnDataDel_803EF8AC, mnDataDel_803EF8C8, and lbl_803EF8D4 to descriptive names.
Check other menu user-data structs for adjacent flag bytes that should be modeled as arrays instead of individual xN fields.
When touching symbols near the edited sdata2 and bss entries, verify local labels such as @8, @156, @203, and ...bss.0 remain correctly scoped.
